pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'You are an Electrical BIM Technician...'.
Explicitly defined as RESULT_FILE in export_result.sh.
setup_task.sh creates this specific file path.
setup_task.sh brief explicitly requests 'Create one distribution board'.
setup_task.sh brief explicitly requests 'Create at least four (4) electrical socket outlets'.
Explicitly defined in both task.json and setup_task.sh.
Cannot be verified because verifier.py is completely missing from the environment.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating an electrical layout on the real-world 'FZK-Haus' IFC file downloaded from ifcwiki.org. The target deliverable /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_electrical.ifc is expected to contain exactly 1 Distribution Board and 4 Socket Outlets, matching the task prompt requirements. However, I discovered two critical defects: 1) `verifier.py` is entirely missing from the environment, rendering the task programmatically unscorable. 2) The starting file 'AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc' is encoded in the IFC4 schema. The task description asks for an 'IfcDistributionBoard', and `export_result.sh` explicitly queries `ifc.by_type('IfcDistributionBoard')`. Because 'IfcDistributionBoard' only exists in the IFC4X3 schema (in IFC4 it was 'IfcElectricDistributionBoard'), ifcopenshell will throw a fatal RuntimeError when parsing an unmodified IFC4 file, forcing all exported metrics to zero unless the agent explicitly upgrades the project schema to IFC4X3.
